disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient has a pre-existing, doctor-diagnosed chronic autoimmune condition causing dry eyes and dry mouth, now acutely worse over 48 hours; this is a recurrent flare rather than a first presentation.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had this before, or any ongoing health conditions?
Is there a name for the dryness condition you mentioned?
Have you been diagnosed with anything by a specialist before?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've had it for a few years — an autoimmune thing that dries out my eyes and mouth. I get flares but this one is much worse than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Ocular symptoms are bilateral and symmetrical; there is no single disproportionately painful eye and no sudden change or loss of vision — a pertinent negative screening for a sight-threatening focal corneal or glaucomatous process.
trigger_question_examples: Is one eye much worse or more painful than the other?
Have you noticed any sudden change or loss of your vision?
Is either eye very painful on its own, or is it both equally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's both eyes the same really — neither one is worse. My vision's a bit blurry from the wateriness but it comes good when I blink; I haven't suddenly lost any sight.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: It's just my eyes, both sore.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no unilateral hot, red, exquisitely painful cheek/jaw swelling with pus from the duct — a pertinent negative screening against acute bacterial parotitis/abscess.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever, or is one cheek hot, red and very painful?
Is the swelling by your ear the same on both sides, or worse on one side?
Have you noticed any bad taste or pus in your mouth from a swollen gland?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever that I've measured, and the puffiness by my ears is the same on both sides — it's not hot or really painful, just a bit full. No pus or bad taste.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unable to swallow solid food today because of severe xerostomia; lives alone with reduced oral intake — a functional flag for dehydration and inadequate nutrition, not an airway or swallowing-mechanism problem.
trigger_question_examples: Have you been able to eat and drink today?
Is anyone at home with you to help while you're unwell?
How are you managing food and fluids with your mouth this dry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I couldn't eat anything solid today — it just won't go down without saliva. I've been sipping water constantly. I'm on my own so it's a struggle.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symmetrical inflammatory-type joint aching and mild swelling of the small joints of both hands and both knees, with profound systemic fatigue — the systemic limb of the exocrine flare.
trigger_question_examples: You mentioned aching — which joints, and are both sides affected?
Are your joints swollen or stiff, and is it the same on both sides?
How is your energy and are the aches in your hands and knees?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My knuckles on both hands and both knees ache and feel a bit puffy and stiff, especially in the morning. And I'm just completely exhausted, more than usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Eyes feel worst on waking, as if the lids are stuck — surrogate for overnight ocular surface drying (nocturnal lagophthalmos), the target of overnight ointment lubrication.
trigger_question_examples: When are your eyes at their worst — any particular time of day?
How do your eyes feel first thing when you wake up?
Do your eyelids feel stuck or scratchy overnight or on waking?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: First thing in the morning is the worst — it feels like my eyelids are stuck to my eyes and it really scratches when I open them.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I just go to the emergency department? It feels really bad and I'm scared about my eyes.
